 {"id":"asupersync-2w6gm","title":"Shard RuntimeState scaffolding","description":"Introduce a sharded RuntimeState layout with independent locks but minimal behavior change.

Scope:
- Define shard structs (TasksState, RegionsState, ObligationsState, FuturesState, TimeState, etc.).
- Replace single Mutex<RuntimeState> with RuntimeState containing internal locks.
- Ensure lock order hooks from bd-20way are applied in any multi-lock accessors.

Acceptance:
- Code compiles with no functional changes beyond lock placement.
- No new dependencies or runtime changes.
- Clear TODOs or follow-ups for remaining refactors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:02.829994977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:14:40.143409529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:14:40.143332246Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-2018r TaskTable extraction and bd-3xvow table split","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2w6gm","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1pj7d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2w8c5","title":"[ALIEN][PLAN] Rewrite rule set + side-condition checker","description":"## Purpose

Encode the lawful rewrites:
- semiring/monoid laws (when declared by policy)
- join/race/timeout algebraic rewrites
- cancellation/obligation laws (loser draining, finalize ordering)

## Deliverables

- Each rule is defined as:
  - pattern -> replacement
  - side conditions (checked)
  - explanation text (for galaxy-brain)

## Acceptance criteria

- Rules are deterministic and only fire when conditions hold.
- Unit tests cover each rule with a small witness plan.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:22.783688540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771355282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771199602Z","external_ref":"bd-2de4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","plan","rewrites"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2w8c5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1vbju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2w8c5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1wl2c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2w8c5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-sskz4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2wab6","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement JSONL exporter for offline replay","description":"Implement the JSONL exporter for the EvidenceLedger in franken_evidence. The exporter writes one JSON object per line to an append-only file, enabling efficient offline replay and analysis of runtime decisions. Implementation: (1) JsonlExporter struct with buffered file writer, (2) Append-only semantics -- never modify existing lines, (3) Atomic flush -- use write + fsync to prevent partial lines on crash, (4) Rotation -- support file rotation by size (default 100MB) or time (default 1 hour), (5) Compression -- optional zstd streaming compression for rotated files, (6) Schema version header -- first line of each file is a schema version record. Performance targets: <1us per record append, <10MB/hour at 1000 decisions/second. Must handle concurrent writers, disk full, and crash recovery. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: jsonl_exporter::write with file_path, entry_count. Span: jsonl_exporter::rotate with reason (size|time), old_file, new_file. DEBUG: JSONL entry written: offset={byte_offset}, size={entry_bytes}, flush_latency_us={lat}. INFO: JSONL file rotated: reason={reason}, old_file={path}, old_size={bytes}, entries_written={n}. WARN: JSONL write latency exceeded threshold: latency_us={lat}, threshold_us={thresh} -- indicates I/O contention. WARN: disk space low: available_bytes={avail}, threshold_bytes={thresh}. ERROR: JSONL write failed: error={err}, dropping entry (no blocking). ERROR: crash recovery: partial last line detected at offset={offset}, skipping {bytes} bytes. Metrics: jsonl_write_latency_us (histogram), jsonl_entries_written_total (counter), jsonl_bytes_written_total (counter), jsonl_rotation_total (counter by reason), jsonl_write_errors_total (counter), jsonl_crash_recovery_skipped_bytes (counter).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:50.548741988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:11:56.020210562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:11:56.020079199Z","external_ref":"bd-qaaxt.2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","foundation","franken-evidence","jsonl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2wab6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qef7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2wab6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-ncytr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-2wbpo","title":"Implement internal Arena<T> utilities for runtime records","description":"# Internal Arena<T> Utilities

## Purpose
Phase 0 needs stable, compact IDs and fast lookup for runtime records:
- regions
- tasks
- obligations

We want the benefits of a slab/arena without bringing in external crates unless necessary.

## Design Requirements
- Deterministic behavior (no hash-randomized iteration relied on for semantics)
- O(1) insert/get/get_mut by ID
- Clear policy about ID reuse (reuse allowed only after safe removal; documented)
- Ergonomic newtype IDs (e.g., `TaskId(u32)`) with explicit conversion

## Plan-of-Record Design
### Arena storage
Use a `Vec<Option<T>>` plus a free list:
- `slots: Vec<Option<T>>`
- `free: Vec<u32>`

Insert:
- if `free` non-empty, pop index and fill
- else push new slot

Remove:
- set slot to None
- push index to free list

### ID types
- `RegionId`, `TaskId`, `ObligationId` are newtypes around `u32`.
- Reserve `0` as root region if desired (explicit constant).

### Safety / invariants
- All public APIs that accept an ID must validate existence (in debug/lab) or be carefully audited.
- Removal rules must be documented:
  - Phase 0 may keep records forever (no removal) to simplify.
  - If we do remove, it must only happen after quiescence/closure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `Arena<T>` supports:
  - `insert -> Id`
  - `get/get_mut`
  - `contains`
  - `iter` for debugging
- Unit tests validate:
  - no accidental reuse while still live
  - removed IDs become invalid
  - insertion after removals reuses indices (if we choose reuse)

## Notes
We should avoid depending on iteration order of arenas for semantics. If order matters for determinism, we must explicitly sort by IDs or use ordered structures.

 {"id":"asupersync-2wel2","title":"Phase 1: Parallel Verification Suite","description":"# Phase 1: Parallel Verification Suite

## Purpose
Extend Phase 0’s verification (oracles, unit tests, E2E scenarios, benchmarks) to cover the parallel scheduler and region heap.

## Required Additions
- Stress tests for work-stealing correctness
- Determinism tests for parallel lab simulation
- Region heap safety tests
- Performance baselines for parallel spawn/scheduling overhead

## Acceptance Criteria
- All Phase 0 tests still pass.
- New parallel tests cover:
  - no duplicate polls
  - no lost tasks
  - cancellation drains under contention
  - region close quiescence under parallel scheduling

 {"id":"asupersync-2wh84","title":"[ALIEN-CS][Scheduler] Power of Two Choices Work Stealing","description":"Replace linear-probe work stealing with the Power of Two Choices randomized load-balancing algorithm.

Background and Rationale:
- The current `steal_task` implementation uses a random starting index and sequentially probes all `Stealer` instances.
- Under high load, linear probing heavily contends on locks (even short-lived ones) and isn't optimal at finding the most congested queue quickly.
- Michael Mitzenmacher's 'Power of Two Choices' (2001) proves that selecting exactly *two* random queues and choosing the lesser-loaded (or in our case, stealing from the *more* loaded) exponentially reduces maximum queue length variance from O(log n / log log n) to O(log log n).

Implementation Constraints & Specifications:
1. **O(1) Length Oracle**: Add an `inline` `len()` method to `Stealer` that briefly locks the queue to read its length without executing a full steal protocol.
2. **Stochastic Control Logic**: In `steal_task`, pick two random, distinct stealers. Query both lengths. Attempt to steal from the one with the larger `len`. 
3. **Fallback Policy**: If the primary choice yields no task (e.g., lock contended or tasks are strictly local/unstealable), immediately attempt the secondary choice. If both fail, gracefully fall back to a linear scan to ensure no work is orphaned.
4. **Isomorphism Proof**: The fundamental invariants of work-stealing (no lost tasks, strict adherence to `is_local` locality flags) must remain intact.

Adoption Wedge:
- In-place replacement of the `steal_task` function since it strictly improves the runtime's internal load-balancing without altering public APIs.

Proof Artifacts & Reproducibility:
- Deterministic test scenarios must demonstrate that with a fixed RNG seed, the correct larger queue is chosen.
- Linear fallback must be proven to engage if both choices fail.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkLynx","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:34:26.197971156Z","created_by":"unknown","updated_at":"2026-03-05T05:41:14.044770787Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T05:41:14.044750419Z","close_reason":"Implemented already (power-of-two steal policy + tests in place); no additional code delta required in this slice.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2wh84","depends_on_id":"asupersync-24q26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:35:42.206070431Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
